title: Into the Breach
edition_pinned: Advanced Edition, 2022-07-19 release era — pins the base combat rules plus five squads, nearly forty weapons, more enemies, bosses, objectives, pilots, and promotion skills.
moat: Enemy intent plus displacement turns each player phase into a plan for redirecting damage before it resolves.

loop_skeleton.description: Inspect attack markers, move three mechs, displace or disable threats, protect buildings through the turn limit, then spend rewards before choosing another mission.

depth_mechanisms.name: telegraphed resolution
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: The interface marks every enemy target and attack type before the player acts; player-turn attacks have no hit roll.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: Hide enemy targets or add hit rolls: players can no longer reproduce a turn outcome from the displayed state, so counterplanning errors cannot be separated from concealed information or chance.

depth_mechanisms.name: displacement as damage routing
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Push, pull, block, smoke, and terrain effects can redirect attacks into enemies or away from buildings, so removing a threat need not mean reducing its health to zero.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: Remove displacement and blocking from a squad: on matched boards, the count of turns solved by redirecting enemy damage falls to zero and attacks converge on health removal.

depth_mechanisms.name: shared grid loss
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Building hits reduce one Power Grid meter across missions, while mechs and mission objectives compete for the same moves used to protect it.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: Reset the Power Grid after each mission: damage accepted to secure an objective stops affecting later mission choice, eliminating the run-level cost of a least-loss turn.

depth_mechanisms.name: run recombination
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Squad rules, pilot skills, weapons, island order, enemies, bosses, and mission objectives recombine across attempts.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: Hold squad, pilot, weapons, island order, enemies, and objectives constant across two attempts: the number of state-dependent plan changes attributable to run setup becomes zero.

feel_signature: See each pending impact, find that one unit, building, or objective must take damage or be abandoned, then choose which cost resolves.
second_session_answer: Return with another squad or island order; known rules produce different displacement chains, upgrade choices, and losses to accept.
packaging_lessons: Ship one screen that exposes targets, attack types, movement, terrain, health, and Grid risk before adding a campaign layer.
Package missions as turn-limited encounters, then let three resources and displayed rewards carry the route choice between them.
anti_lessons: Do not copy telegraphing without displacement, collateral targets, and scarcity; intent markers alone turn combat into arithmetic cleanup.
Do not add a research tree that ignores combat constraints; Subset cut its first strategy layer after it produced complexity without depth.

system_bom.note: A player phase reads attack intent, commits mech actions, and resolves all attacks; this is the counterplanning loop.
Target markers, attack icons, movement previews, and one-screen state make each forecast inspectable before commitment.
Turn limits end missions, Grid depletion ends a timeline, and one pilot can cross into the next attempt.
Objectives and displayed rewards alter which assets the player protects and which losses the player accepts.
Reactor cores, weapons, pilots, and promotion skills change the action set without replacing grid forecasting.
A pilot can cross into the next timeline, and Advanced Edition adds squads and pilot promotion skills to later attempts.
Grid damage persists across missions, and one pilot can cross from a failed timeline into the next attempt.

citations.claim: Edition pin: Advanced Edition is the 2022-07-19 update and adds five squads, nearly forty weapons, enemies, bosses, objectives, pilots, and promotion skills.
citations.source: Subset Games, 'Into the Breach: Advanced Edition' announcement, https://subsetgames.com/itb_ae.html; Subset Games FAQ (release date), https://subsetgames.com/faq.html

citations.claim: Moat: all enemy attacks are shown, player-turn resolution has no hit chance, and the design shifted from killing enemies toward manipulating them.
citations.source: Matthew Davis, 'Into the Breach Design Postmortem,' GDC 2019, slides 13 and 19–21, https://media.gdcvault.com/gdc2019/presentations/Into%20the%20Breach%20Postmortem%20Final.pdf

citations.claim: Depth — telegraphed resolution: enemy attacks are shown and player-turn combat is deterministic.
citations.source: Matthew Davis, 'Into the Breach Design Postmortem,' GDC 2019, slide 13, https://media.gdcvault.com/gdc2019/presentations/Into%20the%20Breach%20Postmortem%20Final.pdf

citations.claim: Depth — displacement as damage routing: the postmortem identifies manipulation over killing and non-lethal weapons as consequences of telegraphed attacks.
citations.source: Matthew Davis, 'Into the Breach Design Postmortem,' GDC 2019, slides 19–21, https://media.gdcvault.com/gdc2019/presentations/Into%20the%20Breach%20Postmortem%20Final.pdf

citations.claim: Depth — shared grid loss: civilian buildings power the mechs and must be defended from both Vek and player fire; the Power Grid became the fail state.
citations.source: Subset Games, 'Into the Breach' product page, https://subsetgames.com/itb.html; Matthew Davis, GDC 2019 postmortem, slides 23–26

citations.claim: Depth — run recombination: attempts generate challenges, while Advanced Edition expands squads, weapons, enemies, bosses, objectives, pilots, and skills.
citations.source: Subset Games, 'Into the Breach' product page, https://subsetgames.com/itb.html; Advanced Edition announcement, https://subsetgames.com/itb_ae.html

citations.claim: Feel signature: displayed attacks support analysis before a counter, while the grid fail state and defensive play force a least-loss choice.
citations.source: Subset Games, 'Into the Breach' product page, https://subsetgames.com/itb.html; Matthew Davis, GDC 2019 postmortem, slides 23–26

citations.claim: Packaging lesson — one-screen forecast: the design uses one-screen UI, target and attack-type icons, orthogonal aiming, static enemy rules, and three attack types.
citations.source: Matthew Davis, 'Into the Breach Design Postmortem,' GDC 2019, slides 27–36, https://media.gdcvault.com/gdc2019/presentations/Into%20the%20Breach%20Postmortem%20Final.pdf

citations.claim: Packaging lesson — mission wrapper: battles use a turn limit; islands expose difficulty through resource count, allow planning, and use three resources.
citations.source: Matthew Davis, 'Into the Breach Design Postmortem,' GDC 2019, slides 21–22 and 42–45, https://media.gdcvault.com/gdc2019/presentations/Into%20the%20Breach%20Postmortem%20Final.pdf

citations.claim: BOM — play-loop and UI: attacks telegraph targets and types on one screen before deterministic player action.
citations.source: Matthew Davis, 'Into the Breach Design Postmortem,' GDC 2019, slides 13 and 27–36, https://media.gdcvault.com/gdc2019/presentations/Into%20the%20Breach%20Postmortem%20Final.pdf

citations.claim: BOM — win-loss-death and save-state: the Power Grid is the fail state, battles have turn limits, and defeat permits one pilot to travel to another timeline.
citations.source: Matthew Davis, GDC 2019 postmortem, slides 21–26; Subset Games product page, https://subsetgames.com/itb.html

citations.claim: BOM — missions: Advanced Edition adds mission objectives, enemies, and bosses to vary run challenges.
citations.source: Subset Games, 'Into the Breach: Advanced Edition' announcement, https://subsetgames.com/itb_ae.html

citations.claim: BOM — progression and meta-progression: runs find weapons and pilots; Advanced Edition expands squads and promotion skills.
citations.source: Subset Games product page, https://subsetgames.com/itb.html; Advanced Edition announcement, https://subsetgames.com/itb_ae.html

citations.claim: Anti-lesson — telegraphing inference: the postmortem ties intent display to manipulation, collateral targets, and scarcity; markers without those constraints do not reproduce its combat decisions.
citations.source: Matthew Davis, 'Into the Breach Design Postmortem,' GDC 2019, slides 19–26: https://media.gdcvault.com/gdc2019/presentations/Into%20the%20Breach%20Postmortem%20Final.pdf

citations.claim: Anti-lesson — cut strategy layer: Subset reports removing a strategy layer after its systems added complexity without supporting the combat design.
citations.source: Matthew Davis, 'Into the Breach Design Postmortem,' GDC 2019, strategy-layer discussion: https://media.gdcvault.com/gdc2019/presentations/Into%20the%20Breach%20Postmortem%20Final.pdf
